intro: Kemulariella abchasica is a plant species of the family Apiaceae, commonly known as a wild celery-like plant native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Kemulariella abchasica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for colds, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
